enonce.texte:
Exercise 1: Match the meals with their correct time.

Relie chaque repas à l'heure à laquelle on le prend.

Bloom's level: Remember (Se souvenir)
images: A daily routine scene showing a person eating breakfast in the morning, lunch at midday, dinner in the evening, and a snack in between meals

questions.texte: Match the meals: breakfast, lunch, dinner, snack
questions.reponse: breakfast = morning, lunch = midday/afternoon, dinner = evening, snack = between meals

sousQuestions.texte: What meal do you eat in the morning?
What meal do you eat in the middle of the day?
What meal do you eat in the evening?

enonce.texte:
Exercise 2: Identify the food groups.

Classe les aliments dans les bonnes catégories.

Aliments : rice, banana, milk, fish, apple, bread, chicken, orange, cheese, carrots

Bloom's level: Understand (Comprendre)
images: A variety of foods arranged in groups: fruits (banana, apple, orange), vegetables (carrots), grains (rice, bread), dairy (milk, cheese), meat/protein (fish, chicken)

questions.texte: Classify the foods into groups: Fruits, Vegetables, Grains, Dairy, Meat/Protein
questions.reponse: Fruits: banana, apple, orange; Vegetables: carrots; Grains: rice, bread; Dairy: milk, cheese; Meat/Protein: fish, chicken

sousQuestions.texte: Which foods are fruits?
Which foods are dairy products?
Which foods are grains?

enonce.texte:
Exercise 3: Choose the correct action verb.

Complète les phrases avec les verbes d'action appropriés.

Verbes : eat, drink, cook, prepare, wash, cut

Bloom's level: Apply (Appliquer)
images: A person in a kitchen performing various actions: washing vegetables, cutting fruits, cooking in a pot, drinking water, preparing a plate, eating at a table

questions.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct action verb.

sousQuestions.texte: I ______ breakfast at 7:00 every morning.
My mother ______ dinner for the family.
We ______ vegetables before cooking them.
I ______ a glass of water every day.
My sister ______ the fruit for the salad.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 4: Write about your breakfast.

Écris 4 phrases pour décrire ce que tu manges au petit-déjeuner.

Utilise les verbes suivants : eat, drink, prepare

Bloom's level: Create (Créer)
images: A breakfast table with bread, butter, fruit, juice, eggs, and tea/coffee

questions.reponse: Example answer: I eat bread and eggs for breakfast. I drink milk every morning. My mother prepares breakfast for me. I have breakfast at 7:00.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 5: Write about your lunch.

Écris 4 phrases pour décrire ce que tu manges au déjeuner.

Utilise les verbes suivants : eat, drink, cook, wash

Bloom's level: Create (Créer)
images: A lunch table with rice, fish, vegetables, a glass of water, and fruit

questions.reponse: Example answer: I eat rice and fish for lunch. I drink water. My mother cooks delicious food. We wash the vegetables before cooking.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 6: Read the menu and answer the questions.

Lis le menu d'une journée équilibrée et réponds aux questions.

🥗 MY BALANCED DAY MENU

Breakfast (7:00): Bread with butter, eggs, orange juice

Morning snack (10:00): A banana

Lunch (12:30): Rice, fish, carrots, salad

Afternoon snack (16:00): An apple

Dinner (19:00): Chicken, vegetables, rice

Bloom's level: Understand (Comprendre) and Analyze (Analyser)
images: A visual menu showing five meals across a day: breakfast with bread and eggs, a banana for snack, lunch with rice and fish, an apple for snack, and dinner with chicken and vegetables

questions.texte: Read the menu and answer the questions.

sousQuestions.texte: What does the person eat for breakfast?
sousQuestions.reponse: Bread with butter, eggs, and orange juice.

sousQuestions.texte: What time does the person have lunch?
What does the person have for the morning snack?
What does the person eat for dinner?
Is this a balanced diet? Why?
sousQuestions.reponse: Yes, because it includes fruits, vegetables, grains, and protein.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 7: Write your daily menu.

Écris 5 phrases pour décrire ce que tu manges dans une journée typique.

Inclus :

  • What you eat for breakfast
  • What you eat for lunch
  • What you eat for dinner
  • What you drink
  • If you have snacks

Bloom's level: Create (Créer)
images: A person writing in a notebook with drawings of food around them: bread, rice, chicken, vegetables, fruits, and water

questions.texte: Write 5 sentences about your daily menu.
questions.reponse: Example answer: For breakfast, I eat bread and eggs and I drink milk. For lunch, I eat rice, fish and vegetables. For dinner, I eat chicken with rice. I drink water with my meals. I eat an apple as a snack.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 8: Compare your menu with a classmate.

Demande à un camarade ce qu'il mange et comparez vos menus.

Questions à poser :

  • What do you eat for breakfast?
  • What do you have for lunch?
  • What is your favourite food?

Bloom's level: Apply (Appliquer) and Evaluate (Évaluer)
images: Two students sitting at a table, talking and comparing their food journals or menus

questions.texte: Interview a classmate and compare your menus.

sousQuestions.texte: What does your classmate eat for breakfast?
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: My classmate eats bread and jam for breakfast.

sousQuestions.texte: What does your classmate have for lunch?
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: He/She has rice and fish for lunch.

sousQuestions.texte: What is your classmate's favourite food?
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: His/Her favourite food is chicken.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 9: Complete the story with action verbs.

Complète l'histoire avec les verbes d'action dans la bonne forme.

Verbes : eat, drink, cook, prepare, wash, cut, boil

Bloom's level: Apply (Appliquer) and Analyze (Analyser)
images: A cooking scene: a person preparing a meal with ingredients on the counter, a pot boiling on the stove

questions.texte: Complete the story with the correct action verbs.

sousQuestions.texte: Every morning, I ______ breakfast for my family.
First, I ______ the vegetables to make them clean.
Then, I ______ the fruit into small pieces.
My mother ______ the rice in a pot.
We ______ together as a family.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 10: Write about your favourite meal.

Écris un paragraphe de 5-6 phrases sur ton repas préféré.

Inclus :

  • What is your favourite meal (breakfast/lunch/dinner)?
  • What do you eat during this meal?
  • Who prepares it?
  • Why do you like it?
  • When do you eat it?

Bloom's level: Create (Créer) and Evaluate (Évaluer)
images: A special meal with a dish that looks delicious, surrounded by family members smiling

questions.texte: Write 5-6 sentences about your favourite meal.
questions.reponse: Example answer: My favourite meal is lunch. I eat rice with fish and vegetables. My mother prepares it for me. I like it because it is very delicious and healthy. I have lunch at 12:30 every day. I drink water with my meal.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 11: Read and decide if the statements are true or false.

Lis chaque phrase et dis si elle est vraie (true) ou fausse (false).

Bloom's level: Understand (Comprendre) and Evaluate (Évaluer)

questions.texte: Decide if the statements are true or false.

sousQuestions.texte: Breakfast is the most important meal of the day.
You should eat only meat and no vegetables.
Drinking water is good for your health.
Fruits and vegetables are not important for a balanced diet.
A balanced diet includes different types of food.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 12: Read the text and answer the questions.

Lis le texte sur les habitudes alimentaires et réponds aux questions.

Text:

"Eating Healthy Every Day

Eating healthy is very important for our body. For breakfast, we should eat bread, eggs, and fruit. We can drink milk or juice. For lunch, we should eat rice, fish, and vegetables. Vegetables give us vitamins. For dinner, we can eat chicken with rice or pasta. We should drink water with our meals. We should not eat too much sugar or fried food. Eating fruits and vegetables makes us strong and healthy!"

Bloom's level: Analyze (Analyser) and Evaluate (Évaluer)
images: A plate with a balanced meal: rice, chicken, vegetables, and a glass of water, with fruits on the side

questions.texte: Read the text and answer the questions.

sousQuestions.texte: What should we eat for breakfast according to the text?
sousQuestions.reponse: We should eat bread, eggs, and fruit.

sousQuestions.texte: What can we drink for breakfast?
sousQuestions.reponse: We can drink milk or juice.

sousQuestions.texte: Why are vegetables important?
What should we not eat too much?
sousQuestions.reponse: We should not eat too much sugar or fried food.

sousQuestions.texte: Why is eating healthy important?
sousQuestions.reponse: Eating healthy is important because it makes us strong and healthy.
